verses.commentary: This is the account of the family line of Esau (that is, Edom). Genealogy chapter.
Esau's wives listed. From Canaan. Canaanite marriages that grieved his parents (26:34-35).
The wife he took to please his father (28:9).
Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau, Basemath bore Reuel.
Five sons total. Born in Canaan.
Moved to land some distance from Jacob. Separation.
Like Abraham and Lot (13:6). Blessing brings separation.
Hill country. Edom's homeland. Fulfilling Isaac's prophecy (27:39-40).
Account of Esau. Father of Edomites. In Seir.
Eliphaz and Reuel. The line continues.
Eliphaz's sons. Five sons.
Amalek becomes Israel's perpetual enemy (Exodus 17:8-16, Deuteronomy 25:17-19).
Reuel's sons. Four sons.
Oholibamah's sons. Three sons. Third line.
These were the chiefs among Esau's descendants: The sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau: Chiefs Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz.
More chiefs. Including Amalek. Leadership in Edom.
These were the chiefs descended from Reuel in Edom; they were grandsons of Esau's wife Basemath. Four chiefs.
These were the chiefs descended from Esau's wife Oholibamah daughter of Anah. Three chiefs.
These were the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and these were their chiefs. Summary statement. Sons of Esau. That is Edom. Their chiefs.
Living in region. Indigenous people.
More Horite sons. They were chiefs.
Lotan's sons. The Timna who bore Amalek (verse 12).
Shobal's sons. Five sons. Horite genealogy.
Zibeon's sons. Unique detail.
Anah's children. Oholibamah is Esau's wife (verse 2).
Dishon's sons. Four sons.
Ezer's sons. Three sons.
Dishan's sons. Two sons.
Native leadership.
More Horite chiefs. Tribal structure.
These were the kings who reigned in Edom before any Israelite king reigned. Kings in Edom. This shows Edom's early political development.
City: Dinhabah.
Bela died. Jobab succeeded. From Bozrah. Second king. Non-dynastic succession (son doesn't inherit).
Jobab died. Husham succeeded. Third king. Still not dynastic.
Husham died. Hadad succeeded. City: Avith. Fourth king.
Hadad died. Samlah succeeded. From Masrekah. Fifth king.
Samlah died. Shaul succeeded. Sixth king.
Shaul died. Seventh king.
Baal-Hanan died. Hadad succeeded. City: Pau. Wife: Mehetabel. Eighth king.
Chiefs from Esau.
Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon. More chiefs.
Important Edomite center.
Final chiefs. Magdiel and Iram. Father of Edomites.
